channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: President Trump seeks control of science funding
item.description: The White House Office of Management and Budget is moving to take more control of billions of dollars in federal grants. Critics say the proposed change would jeopardize the integrity of U.S. science.
item.content:encoded:
The White House Office of Management and Budget is moving to take more control of billions of dollars in federal grants. Critics say the proposed change would jeopardize the integrity of U.S. science.

(Image credit: Andrew Harnik)
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: Voters in 6 states went to the polls Tuesday. Takeaways from the primaries
item.description: Voters in six states cast ballots Tuesday in primary races for U.S. House, Senate and statewide offices. A look at the results.
item.content:encoded: Voters in six states cast ballots Tuesday in primary races for U.S. House, Senate and statewide offices. A look at the results.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: The winners and losers from Tuesday's primaries
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel sizes up the results of six states' primaries with Matthew Klein of the Cook Political Report.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel sizes up the results of six states' primaries with Matthew Klein of the Cook Political Report.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: We finally know enough about how the brain breaks to focus on fixing it, experts say
item.description: The Allen Institute in Seattle says scientists have now learned enough about how the brain works to start fixing it when it breaks.
item.content:encoded:
The Allen Institute in Seattle says scientists have now learned enough about how the brain works to start fixing it when it breaks.

(Image credit: Erik Dinnel)
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: 'They went through my vocal cords': Barry Manilow recovers after lung cancer surgery
item.description: Nearly 83, Barry Manilow is recovering from cancer, preparing for a series of concerts, and releasing his first album of new songs in nearly 15 years, What a Time.
item.content:encoded:
Nearly 83, Barry Manilow is recovering from cancer, preparing for a series of concerts, and releasing his first album of new songs in nearly 15 years, What a Time.

(Image credit: Christopher DeVargas)
item.dc:creator: Phil Harrell

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: A look at the results from Tuesday's primaries, acting AG says Justice Department is scrapping controversial "anti-weaponization" fund, housing official named acting national intelligence director.
item.content:encoded: A look at the results from Tuesday's primaries, acting AG says Justice Department is scrapping controversial "anti-weaponization" fund, housing official named acting national intelligence director.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: '60 Minutes' correspondent Scott Pelley fired amid rising tensions at CBS News
item.description: Scott Pelley, the longtime "60 Minutes" correspondent, was fired from CBS a day after a heated staff meeting exchange with the show's new executive producer.
item.content:encoded: Scott Pelley, the longtime "60 Minutes" correspondent, was fired from CBS a day after a heated staff meeting exchange with the show's new executive producer.
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: Secretary of State Marco Rubio faces questions about Iran war on Capitol Hill
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is making the rounds on Capitol Hill for budget hearings, facing questions about how the war in Iran will end and when gas prices for Americans might return to normal.
item.content:encoded: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is making the rounds on Capitol Hill for budget hearings, facing questions about how the war in Iran will end and when gas prices for Americans might return to normal.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: Trump administration shares new work requirements for Medicaid recipients
item.description: The Trump administration this week released guidance for states on the work rules many Americans on Medicaid will need to abide by in order to be eligible for benefits on Jan. 1.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration this week released guidance for states on the work rules many Americans on Medicaid will need to abide by in order to be eligible for benefits on Jan. 1.
item.dc:creator: Julie Rovner

item.title: Sen. Warner warns of risks from Pulte's intelligence appointment
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Sen. Mark Warner of Virginia, the top Democrat on the Senate Intelligence committee, about mortgage chief Bill Pulte's move to acting director of national intelligence.
item.content:encoded:
NPR's Steve Inskeep asks Sen. Mark Warner of Virginia, the top Democrat on the Senate Intelligence committee, about mortgage chief Bill Pulte's move to acting director of national intelligence.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep
